nav.pin_study: Pin a study for quick access.

home.title: Home
home.lede: One place for every active study, draft, and decision.

first_study_hero.title: Design your first study.
first_study_hero.body: Merism recruits, interviews, codes, and reports on one focused research question end-to-end.

studies.title: Studies
studies.lede: Everything you're learning about your users.
studies.empty_hero_body: A study is one focused line of research. Merism recruits, interviews, codes, and reports on it end-to-end.

ask.title: Ask Merism
ask.lede: A conversational co-researcher across your entire workspace.
ask.empty_hero_title: What do you want to know?
ask.empty_hero_body: Ask questions across every study, session, and quote. Merism searches the knowledge base and cites sources inline.
suggestions: What do participants say about pricing?
ask.placeholder: Ask Merism anything about your research…
ask.saved_coming_soon: Pin your favourite answers to re-run them later.

inbox.title: Inbox
inbox.lede: New responses, notifications, and signals across your studies.
inbox.empty_body: New session completions, channel alerts, and @-mentions will land here.

repository.title: Repository
repository.lede: Every quote, theme, and artefact — searchable across studies.
repository.empty_body: As studies complete, Merism indexes every quote and code for cross-study search.

decisions.title: Decisions
decisions.lede: Action items extracted from research, tracked to close.
decisions.empty_body: Action items from completed sessions appear here with evidence links.

settings.title: Settings
settings.lede: Workspace, team, and your account preferences.

workspace.title: Workspace details
workspace.members_description: Team members with access to this workspace.
workspace.members_body: Member management lands with the first multi-tenant rollout.
workspace.compliance_body: PIPL-aligned. Raw transcripts kept server-side; clean-verbatim exports available from any session.

login.heading: Welcome back.
login.sub: Sign in to your research workspace.

interview.session_ended: Thank you. Your session is complete.
interview.session_ended_body: Your response has been recorded. You may close this window.

errors.not_found_body: The page you're looking for doesn't exist or has moved.

insights.title: Insights
insights.select_study_desc: Choose a study to view its AI-generated research insights.
insights.no_insights_desc: Insights will be generated once interviews are completed.
insights.generation_failed_desc: Something went wrong while generating insights. Please try again.

reports.title: Reports
reports.select_study_desc: Choose a study to create and view custom AI research reports.
reports.no_reports_desc: Create a custom report to ask new research questions of your interview data.

participant.something_wrong_desc: This invitation link may have expired or the study has closed.

errors.link_invalid_desc: This link is not valid. Please check the URL.
errors.study_closed_desc: This study is no longer accepting participants.
errors.study_full_desc: This study has reached its participant limit.
errors.invitation_required_desc: You need a personal invitation to join this study.
errors.consent_required_desc: You must agree to the consent form to continue.
errors.interview_not_ready_desc: The interview is not yet available.
errors.generic_desc: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.

interview_room.text_placeholder: Type if you'd rather not speak…

analysis.title: Analysis
analysis.custom_report_placeholder: What do you want to know?

outline.question_text_placeholder: Write the prompt that the participant will see or hear.
outline.question_type_helper: Switching type swaps the configuration block below.
outline.probing_placeholder: What should the interviewer probe for?
outline.allow_skip_helper: Useful when the participant already answered the signal elsewhere.
outline.allow_dynamic_probe: Allow interviewer to dynamically probe once they finish the task
outline.allow_dynamic_probe_helper: Lets the moderator ask follow-up questions after completion.
outline.allow_skip_task_helper: Participants can move on if the task is not appropriate.
outline.randomize_options_helper: Keeps the response list fresh during repeated sessions.
outline.other_option_helper: Adds a custom response path when the listed choices do not fit.
outline.allow_skip_select_helper: Use when the question should be optional in live interviews.
outline.ask_explanation_helper: The moderator will ask why the participant chose that score.
outline.allow_skip_rating_helper: Makes the rating optional in live moderation.
outline.allow_decimal_helper: Use when the answer may contain fractions or percentages.
outline.allow_skip_number_helper: Makes the number question optional in the live interview.
outline.review_desc: Six axes: privacy · ordering · structure · bias · alignment · follow-up depth
outline.review_placeholder: Ask me to review anything specific…

brief.research_goal_placeholder: Why do advanced users cancel after day 14?

recruit.participant_criteria_placeholder: e.g. Chinese working professionals aged 25-40 who use productivity apps daily and have used at least 2 competing tools in the past 6 months.

screener.summary_subtitle: Pass criteria close the loop before save.

study_settings.research_objectives_desc: The concrete questions this study aims to answer.
study_settings.objective_placeholder: e.g. Understand what was confusing about the checkout flow…

stimuli.no_stimuli: No stimuli available — upload one first

glossary.title: Glossary
glossary.description: Term replacements applied during transcript cleaning. Useful for ASR-likely misrecognitions of product names, people, or jargon.

llm_gateway.providers_desc: LLM providers available to this team. Credentials are encrypted at rest.
llm_gateway.routes_desc: Map logical capabilities to providers. Each team can have one route per capability.
llm_gateway.budget_desc: Monthly spend tracking per team. Reconciled hourly from Langfuse.
